short: Pairing a retriever with a generator so answers draw on fetched documents, not just weights.
definition: Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines a search step, typically a vector database (a store that finds text by meaning rather than exact words), with a text-generating model. Matching passages are inserted into the model's prompt so it can reason over and cite up-to-date or specialized information it did not learn during training. This reduces hallucination (confident but made-up answers), lets knowledge be updated without retraining, and provides traceability through source citations.
